claim_boundary: TICKET127 proves four exact intermediate statements, including one public correction, but proves or refutes none of RH, Collatz, Goldbach, or Twin Prime.

riemann.setting: Let V be a separable topological vector space, let Q:V->R be continuous, and let D={d_0,d_1,...} be an explicitly enumerable dense core. Normalize the exact RH-equivalent Weil criterion so that RH requires Q(g)>=0 for every admissible g in V.
riemann.proved_statement: If an admissible g has Q(g)<0, then some enumerated core element d_j has Q(d_j)<0. If interval evaluation is complete for strict negative values, dovetailed core enumeration semidecides a counterexample to the criterion.
riemann.proof: Continuity makes Q^{-1}((-infinity,0)) open. A negative witness g therefore has an open neighborhood contained in the negative set. Density gives d_j in that neighborhood. A complete strict-sign interval evaluator eventually returns an upper endpoint below zero for d_j, so dovetailing eventually halts on every real violation.
effective_assumptions: an exact RH-equivalent Weil criterion with one fixed sign convention
a proved dense enumerable core inside the completed admissible space
validated interval evaluation complete for every strict negative core value

finite_sanity_model.space: R^2 with Q(x,y)=x^2-y^2 and dense core Q^2

route_decision.retain: interval-certified enumeration as an RH-counterexample semidecision route
route_decision.discard: treating finite failure to find a negative core element as a proof of RH

riemann.proof_boundary: The theorem makes counterexample search complete under its explicit topological and effective assumptions. It supplies neither the dense Weil core nor a negative witness, and finite non-discovery proves no RH case.

collatz.corrected_statement: For every positive odd integer n>1, n has no finite strict Collatz descent if and only if its compatible residue path is a nontrivial eventually-low infinite path in the adaptive unresolved tree.
collatz.proof: Apply the exact TICKET126 path equivalence to n>1. TICKET125 proves that every n>1 has a finite strict descent exactly when Collatz holds. The fixed n=1 path is eventually low and survives every tree level, but it belongs to the known terminal basin and is not a counterexample.

historical_correction.error: The original Collatz corollary said that all eventually-low paths must be absent, omitting the necessary n>1 qualifier.
historical_correction.repair: Retain the exact path equivalence, explicitly keep the n=1 path, and exclude only nontrivial eventually-low paths.
historical_correction.scientific_effect: The old exclusion target was impossible because n=1 is a permanent witness. The corrected target is logically equivalent to Collatz.

route_decision.discard: the impossible target of excluding the fixed n=1 path

collatz.proof_boundary: The equivalence and correction are exact. The finite 28-bit tree still leaves nontrivial classes and does not prove their uniform exclusion.

goldbach.definition: For even N, S(N)=2*C2*product_{p|N,p>2}(p-1)/(p-2), where C2=product_{p>2}(1-1/(p-1)^2). Define the exact residual R(N)=G(N)-S(N)N.
goldbach.proved_statement: S(N)>=1 for every positive even N.
goldbach.proof: Every correction factor (p-1)/(p-2) is at least one. For M>=2, the prime-indexed factors of C2 form a subset of the factors 1-1/m^2, 2<=m<=M. All factors lie in (0,1), so the prime subset product is at least the full product. The latter telescopes to (M+1)/(2M)>1/2. Taking the convergent decreasing prime-product limit gives C2>=1/2 and hence S(N)>=1.

exact_normalization_consequence.scope: This closes only the singular-series coefficient after R is defined against the exact Hardy-Littlewood model. It proves no residual estimate.

route_decision.remaining_premise: prove |R(N)|<=K*N/log(N) pointwise for every even N>H with an explicit K below the endpoint ceiling

goldbach.proof_boundary: The local singular-series factor is no longer an unknown constant. The pointwise binary Goldbach residual is the unresolved analytic core, so Goldbach remains open.

twin_prime.proved_statement: For K_X,K_2X>0 and Q_X=A_X/K_X, the normalized inequality Q_2X<=alpha*Q_X+beta is equivalent to the raw transport inequality A_2X<=alpha*(K_2X/K_X)*A_X+beta*K_2X.
twin_prime.proof: Multiply the normalized inequality by positive K_2X and substitute Q_X=A_X/K_X. Division by K_2X proves the reverse implication.
twin_prime.sufficient_growth_corollary: If A_2X<=u*A_X+v*K_2X, K_2X>=gamma*K_X, K_X>0, and A_X,u>=0, gamma>0, then Q_2X<=(u/gamma)Q_X+v.

route_decision.retain: coefficient-level raw numerator and denominator transport
route_decision.discard: inferring a uniform tail recurrence from five finite endpoint transitions

twin_prime.proof_boundary: The theorem exactly exposes what a normalized recurrence means in raw Vaughan budgets. The finite audit proves no uniform coefficient transport, all-X interpolation, parity breakthrough, or Twin Prime theorem.

literature_boundary.citation: Connes and Consani, The Scaling Hamiltonian
literature_boundary.role: Primary Weil-autocorrelation criterion context; no RH positivity theorem is imported.

literature_boundary.citation: Tao, Almost all orbits of the Collatz map attain almost bounded values
literature_boundary.role: Primary almost-all boundary; the corrected target remains universal over n>1.

literature_boundary.citation: Friedlander, Goldston, Iwaniec, and Suriajaya, Exceptional zeros and the Goldbach problem
literature_boundary.role: Primary source for the binary Goldbach singular-series normalization.

literature_boundary.citation: Ford and Maynard, On the theory of prime producing sieves
literature_boundary.role: Primary sieve context; no exact-gap parity theorem is imported.
